comments.body: The changelog in the .spec needs a bump, not just the package version. There is a CI test for this.
Well as soon as I sent the LGTM, the Travis CI flipped from green to red on my screen....

Looks like the version.bats tests aren't happy with something.

```
not ok 160 buildah version current in .spec file Version
# (in test file ./version.bats, line 15)
#   `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]' failed
not ok 161 buildah version current in .spec file changelog
# (in test file ./version.bats, line 21)
#   `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]' failed
/home/travis/.travis/job_stages: line 169: 24709 Terminated              travis_jigger $! $timeout $cmd
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat, it's because the output from `buildah version` doesn't match what's in the changelog / spec file, which is correct behaviour for the test.

The version going in this commit should be stamped 1.1, then another commit to bump to 1.2? The 1.2 test will fail in this case because there is no 1.2 update to the RPM spec or RPM changelog.

-edit-

Can we just do 1.2 as a different PR?
The most recent changelog entry in the spec needs to be 1.2 to pass.
@pixdrift I am trying to allow adding -dev to version number so we can identify when people are testing from Master branch versus something we shipped.

@rhatdan, that's fine. But the check is checking that the latest changelog entry matches the version number returned by buildah. In this commit the version is 1.2-dev, but the latest commit in the spec file is 1.1-1
It's quite peculiar that CentOS 7 and Fedora 27 passed on that RPM version check, but Fedora 28 failed.
